---
title: "Ubuntu 14.04 running on Macbook 2010 Freezes and Buggy Graphics"
date: 2016-01-14
forum: Apple Hardware Users
---

### Post by Red_Dark on 2016-01-14
[LINK TO MACBOOK SPECS]("https://support.apple.com/kb/sp584?locale=en_US")

I installed Ubuntu 14.04 on my Macbook 2010 a few days ago from an install CD i found laying around my dads office. I really didnt like OSX so i said why not. It worked fine for the first few minutes of running it, but after that, it seems to completely freeze up, excluding the mouse. The computer freezes, not letting me click on anything that is going on. I use this computer for school so I need it to work for more than 5 minutes at a time. Above I linked the specs to my computer. They are as is, and I have not modified anything internally. I don't have any important data on it, so if a HD wipe is an idea that might work, please feel free to suggest it. 

My second problem is that the graphics are slightly buggy. On boot up, where it would display the Ubuntu logo, it just has a jumbled mess of purlple/black/white/orangle lines. [THIS IS WHAT IT IS.]("http://data:image/jpeg;base64,") It does a similar thing when I get to the actual desktop. Terminal lines will look similar to this, and so does everything else.

---

### Post by howefield on 2016-01-14
Thread moved to the "*Apple Hardware Users*" forum.

### Post by kyle69 on 2016-01-14
You might want to try Lubuntu 14.04 instead, it would be very quick and easier to configure with your hardware. I have a older mini that runs like a champ with Lubuntu and I prefer the simpler UI.

---

### Post by ben147 on 2016-01-17
Cheers, I came across this post! I love Lubuntu and have it running on several Laptops and my home PC with two Apple Cinema Displays. I also own an iMac from 2010 and one of the last 17 inch Macbook Pro's and I would like to ask for some advice in installing Lubuntu on both of them. I am not planning to do it right away but wanted to check if it is possible in principle. Ben
